title: MailerLite vs GetResponse (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and GetResponse: pricing, features, and performance. Find the best email marketing tool for your needs.
intro: Both MailerLite and GetResponse are popular email marketing platforms offering freemium models, but they serve different user needs. MailerLite excels at simplicity and affordability for small businesses, while GetResponse offers a broader suite of marketing tools for online entrepreneurs. This comparison will help you choose the right platform based on your specific requirements.
choose_a_if: You want a simple, intuitive email marketing tool focused on essentials without overwhelming features
You're on a tight budget and need the lowest entry price point with a generous free tier
You run an e-commerce business and need strong integration capabilities for online stores
choose_b_if: You need a comprehensive all-in-one platform that includes webinars and online course hosting
You want advanced conversion funnel building and sales automation tools
You're comfortable with a higher starting price in exchange for more sophisticated marketing features

faq.a: It depends on your use case. MailerLite is better for those prioritizing simplicity and affordability, with higher user satisfaction ratings (4.6 vs 4.2 on G2). GetResponse is better if you need webinars, online courses, and advanced funnel-building capabilities. Neither is objectively 'better'—it's about fit.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or GetResponse?
faq.a: MailerLite is significantly cheaper, starting at $9/month versus GetResponse's $19/month. MailerLite also offers a more generous free tier with 1,000 subscribers compared to GetResponse's 500. For budget-conscious users, MailerLite provides better value.

faq.q: Can I switch from GetResponse to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, switching is possible. Both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and email campaigns, though some formatting may need adjustment. MailerLite has import features to help move your contacts, and you can often export from GetResponse in standard CSV format for easier migration.
